'''Shogun''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] conquered by and aligned with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]].

==Description==
Shogun was a world of surreal, [[Crystal/Legends|crystalline]] terrain that formed vistas described as "dreamscapes."

==History==
{{Quote|I put Shysa out of his misery. We were surrounded, he was too badly hurt to escape, and I couldn't leave him to the Sevvets.|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], to [[Goran Beviin]]|<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>}}
Shogun was conquered by one of the ancient [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalores]] years before the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. According to legend, [[Mandalore the Indomitable]] had a vision while on the world, which provided him with a realization of great importance to [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] theology. He returned to [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] and told his clansmen of it. [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] historians connected this vision with the subsequent shift in the [[Mandalorian religion]] from worshiping the Destroyer God [[Kad Ha'rangir]], to worshiping war itself.<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

Later, Mandalore the Indomitable's successor, [[Mandalore the Ultimate]], also had a vision on Shogun. This led him to decree that non-[[Taung/Legends|Taungs]] would be admitted to Mandalorian clans if they proved themselves worthy in battle and upheld Mandalorian honor.<ref name="Warfare" />

In [[21 ABY/Legends|21 ABY]], [[Fenn Shysa]] died on Shogun saving [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett's]] life. [[Ailyn Vel]] later found Shysa's corpse on the planet, as well as the ''[[Slave IV]]''.<ref name="I80" />

{{Planet-stub}}
